summary: This patient presents signs suggestive of either Wilson’s Disease or Hallervorden-Spatz syndrome due to extensive neurodegenerative pattern involving multiple regions; however, based on broad regional impact noted especially outside typical core areas like basal ganglia, Hallervorden-Spatz appears as most probable cause for such diverse presentation among pediatric patients exhibiting these symptoms through MR imaging results indicating wide spread pathology affecting deep gray nuclei along with cerebral/cerebellar tracts/white matters bilaterally albeit unevenly distributed throughout central nervous system tissues examined via magnetic resonance images obtained from this young individual experiencing atypical motor behaviors indicative thereof clinically too possibly secondary to progressive neuronal loss coupled with demyelination processes occurring concurrently within affected neural pathways identified radiologically herein reported by clinicians observing secured prior commencement of diagnostic workup procedures ordered then followed up closely thereafter over ensuing days until final determination could be made regarding underlying etiology
